english: v. dgár-ba (dgar ba).
v. ,kál-ba ('khal ba) and ,gél-ba ('gel ba).
instr. of bka (bka'); bkás-pa (bkas pa) v. ,gés-pa ('ges pa).
Lex.: to extract, to make an extract of a drug by drawing out the juice (kú-ba byin-pa (khu ba byin pa)); bkús-te bór-ba (bkus te bor ba) id.; smán-bku (sman bku) medicinal extract.
v. kug (khug); bkúg-pa (bkug pa) v. ,gúgs-pa ('gugs pa).
v kum (khum); bkúm-pa (bkum pa) v. ,gúms-pa ('gums pa).

I. 1. vb. to honour, esteem (synon. mčód-pa (mchod pa)), máṅ-pos bkúr-bai rgyál-po (mang pos bkur ba'i rgyal po), महासम्मत, the king honoured by many, frq.; kún-gyis bkúr-žiṅ mčód-pai ,os (kun gyis bkur zhing mchod pa'i 'os) worthy of general honour and respect Mil.; mis bkúr-bar mi ,gyur (mis bkur bar mi 'gyur) is not esteemed by men Dzl. — 2. sbst. honour; more frq., bkúr sti (bkur sti), honour, respect, homage, mark of honour, bkúr-stis mčód-pa (bkur stis mchod pa) to distinguish (a person) by marks of respect Zam.; ráṅ-la bkúr-stí ,byuṅ dus (rang la bkur sti 'byung dus) when honour is shown to yourself Mil.; bkúr-sti byéd pa (bkur sti byed pa) to do honour frq.; to make a reverence, to salute. — II. pf. of ,kúr-ba ('khur ba) to carry; in the term máṅ-pos bkúr-bai rgyál-po (mang pos bkur ba'i rgyal po) the legend combines this signification with the preceding one Glr.
v. ,góg-pa ('gog pa).
v. ,góṅ-ba ('gong ba).
v. ,gód-pa ('god pa).
v ,kón-pa ('khon pa).
(*kób-ta* (*kob ta*)) the plan of an undertaking W. (vulg. pronunciation for bkod-blta? (bkod blta?))
Mil. seems to be a kind of goblins.
v. kól-ba (khol ba).
Cs.: to talk nonsense, v. kyál-ka (khyal ka); bkyál-pai ṅag = kyál-ka (bkyal pa'i ngag = khyal ka) Lex.
v. ,kyíg-pa ('khyig pa).
v. ,gyéd-pa ('gyed pa).
to bend back, recline (vb. nt.), rgyaṅs byéd-de (rgyangs byed de) bending or turning far aside.
to beat (= rdúṅ-ba (rdung ba)) Mil. nt.; bka bkyon-pa (bka' bkyon pa) resp. to chastise with words, to scold, frq. (v. bka (bka'), phrases); Schr. mentions also bkyon-bkyál (bkyon bkyal) chiding.

(Lex.: = चित्र, cf. also krá-bo (khra bo)) 1. variegated. — 2. beautiful, blooming (of complexion); glossy, well-fed (of animals); ša-bkrá (sha bkra) n. of a cutaneous disease. Comp. bkra-bzáṅ (bkra bzang) n. of a mountain in Tibet. — bkra-lam-mé (bkra lam me) v. kra-lam-mé (khra lam me). — bkra-šis (bkra shis) Ssk. मङ्गल 1. happiness, prosperity, blessing, ,báṅs-rnams-la bkra-šis šog ('bangs rnams la bkra shis shog) happiness to my people! may they prosper! Glr.; *nád-med tsád-med ṭa-ši pún-sum-tsóg žu* (*nad med tshad med ta shi phun sum tshog zhu*) I wish you good health and immeasurable and perfect happiness! (new year's wish in W.); bkra-šís-kyi ču (bkra shis kyi chu) holy water Glr.; bkra-šís-kyi mál-kri (bkra shis kyi mal khri) nuptial bed Cs.; bkra šis-kyi tsig (bkra shis kyi tshig) or smon-lam (smon lam) blessing, benediction; *ṭa-ší šig!* (*ta shi shig!*) Good bye! May you be happy! *kyód-la ṭa-ši čo!* (*khyod la ta shi cho!*) I wish you joy! (also ironically) W.; bkra-šis-šiṅ (bkra shis shing) being happy, enjoying prosperity Glr.; bkra-šis srúṅ-bai gó-ča (bkra shis srung ba'i go cha) instruments used for insuring happiness (to a new-born infant) Lt. — 2. sacrificial ceremony by which blessings are to be drawn down, bkra-šis byéd-pa (bkra shis byed pa) or mčód-pa (mchod pa) or *yóg-če* (*yog che*) (W., barley being scattered — γyog-pa (g.yog pa) — on that occasion), to perform this ceremony, — bkra-šis-pa (bkra shis pa) propitious, lucky, perh. also: happy; bkra-šis-pai ltas (bkra shis pa'i ltas) lucky signs; bkra-šis-pai rtags (bkra shis pa'i rtags) lucky configurations or semblances (such as e. g. devout imagination seeks to discover in the outlines of mountains etc. Glr. fol. 58.) bkra-šis-ma (bkra shis ma) n. of a goddess, Sch.: goddess of glory Dzl. — bkra-mi-šis (bkra mi shis) misfortune, calamity, bkra-mi-šís-pa (bkra mi shis pa) un-
